title: [FIRRTL][LowerClasses] Handle wires of properties
body:
Input:

```
FIRRTL version 4.0.0

circuit WireProp:
  module WireProp:
    input in : String
    output out : String

    wire s : String
    propassign s, in
    propassign out, s
```

Current error:
```
wire-prop.fir:8:5: error: failed to legalize operation 'firrtl.wire' that was explicitly marked illegal
    wire s : String
    ^
wire-prop.fir:8:5: note: see current operation: %0 = "firrtl.wire"() {annotations = [], name = "s", nameKind = #firrtl<name_kind droppable_name>} : () -> !firrtl.string
```

The above simple case can be canonicalized away by fixing/implementing #6049 , but there will still be wires in some scenarios (and passes my insert them at some point).

Haven't looked too closely, but in case this helps:

Due to single-connect nature of propassign, chasing through wires on one side (source, since we filter on those with dest of output port?) and collapsing might be enough? Graph regions save the def-before-use concerns, hopefully.
